title: Revenue Guard
summary: Quote-approval guardrail that catches discount creep, unpaid services, and cash drag before signature.
intent_examples: show me the deal desk demo

scenario.trigger: Procurement asks for deeper discounting, custom SLA coverage, and slower payment terms in the same revision.
scenario.operator_goal: Protect gross margin while keeping the deal winnable.

ctx_context.exact_method: Exact tokenizer battery across openai-o200k, openai-cl100k, llama3, gemma, deepseek, glm.
ctx_context.effective_context_note: Nominal fit is not effective fit: context remains a sequential computational substrate, so retrieval quality and token position still matter.

scientific_view.claim: Quote governance becomes faster when pricing, services, and cash deltas share one executable context.
scientific_view.confirmation: Revenue Guard compresses 621-626 natural tokens into 250-315 codec tokens across the tokenizer battery, yielding 1.98x-2.5x compression while preserving the guardrail verdict and economics. Best codec family: llama3. Worst codec family: gemma. Nominal fit is not effective fit: context remains a sequential computational substrate, so retrieval quality and token position still matter.

functional_gain.operator_surface_before: quote doc + finance notes + Slack exceptions

recommendations: Cap the discount at 20% unless the custom SLA is billed as a separate premium line item.
Recover onboarding cost explicitly instead of burying it inside ARR.
Hold payment terms at 45 days or require annual prepay to offset working-capital drag.

economic_case.headline: $60,200 gross profit recovered and $11,096 less cash drag with a guarded quote revision.

output.rationale: The requested revision combines price leakage, unfunded service load, and cash delay into one unattractive deal shape.
